message:
audit(btree): wire cell-slot cache hit/miss counters + workload audit

bd-k7zd7 audit: add global Relaxed AtomicU64 counters inside
CellSlotCache::get plus a cell_slot_cache_counter_snapshot /
reset_cell_slot_cache_counters pair so any workload can read ground-truth
hit/miss data for the bd-rrfvc cache. Ships ignored
audit_cell_slot_cache_hit_rate_write_vs_read bench that drives six
representative scenarios.

Measured on MemPageStore, 10 000 rows, 35-byte payload, release build:

| scenario                    | hits   | misses | total   | hit-rate |
|-----------------------------|-------:|-------:|--------:|---------:|
| monotonic_append            |      0 |      1 |       1 |    0.00% |
| random_insert               |      0 |     12 |      12 |    0.00% |
| read_heavy_after_append     |      0 |      0 |       0 |        — |
| reverse_seek_preloaded      |      0 |      0 |       0 |        — |
| delete_all_after_append     |     98 | 10 000 |  10 098 |    0.97% |
| index_random_insert         | 64 416 | 66 852 | 131 268 |   49.07% |

Key finding: parse_cell_at is effectively cold on the table-insert hot
path — try_append_on_cached_rightmost_leaf + table_seek_for_insert's
interpolation search bypass it entirely (at most 12 calls per 10 000
inserts). The 8.62% self-time in the 2026-04-23 MT 8t profile was likely
inflated by the pre-4b061dcc interior child_page_at traffic, since
removed. Actual hot callers on current HEAD are DELETE predecessor /
separator helpers (99% miss; mutation_counter bump invalidates after
every delete) and index-btree binary search (49% hit on 13 calls/op).

Bench:  cargo test -p fsqlite-btree --release --lib
        audit_cell_slot_cache_hit_rate_write_vs_read -- --ignored --nocapture
Parity: 363/364 btree lib tests pass; single failure is the pre-existing
        prop_index_btree_structural_invariants_hold_after_random_mutations,
        unrelated to this change.
Bead:   bd-k7zd7 (findings + recommendations attached as comment)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
